summary: Add a toggle for the `Automatically open successfully exported files` setting to the toolbar's export panel

description.text: ### Description First off, thank you so much for the new ability to check/uncheck `Automatically open successfully exported files` in `Preferences > Import and Export`. This has already been a major quality of life improvement while using Zettlr on Linux, as the editor would previously spawn a new PDF application window on every export, which could mean dozens of extraneous windows needing to be closed over the course of tweaking the look of an exported document. The only additional enhancement I would ask for in this regard is to perhaps add a checkbox or toggle switch for the setting to the toolbar's export panel. E.g., This is because, while the new feature eliminates some great annoyances, there are still times when it's helpful to open the exported file automatically. For example, the _first_ time I export something, I'd like the exported document to open in the external viewer. It's on subsequent exports that the auto-export becomes troublesome, as it's spawning duplicate copies of the document I already have open in my PDF viewer. Being able to toggle the functionality right from the export panel would make it that much simpler to turn on open-externally when I first export a file and then flip it back to off on subsequent exports. Toggling the feature from the main settings menu is welcome functionality, but because the decision to launch or not launch an external viewer is often contextually specific to the immediate document I'm working on, and whether I already have it open in an external viewer, it can feel a bit weird to toggle a global setting. ### Proposed Changes See above. ### Caveats None. ### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself? No ### Zettlr Version Nightly ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Operating System Version Manjaro (Rolling) ### Additional Information Thank you!!!

summary: Option to count characters including spaces

description.text:
### Description

Currently, characters are counted without spaces. Thus it is not easy to find out the length of anything including blank spaces, except by calculating it by hand using the provided ranges. Whether spaces are counted or discounted is a matter of preference, depending on the guidelines in place.

### Proposed Changes

Zettlr should offer the option to choose whether character counts are displayed with or without spaces.

### Caveats

If "count without spaces" is the default, there are no caveats. In this case, nothing changes if counting spaces isn't actively chosen.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: User Dictionaries are not saved safely on application exit.

description.text:
### Description

The current way Zettlr saves the User Dictionary (`user.dic` file) is not data secure, and is easily prone to being corrupted, or zeroed out. 

Currently, Zettlr writes to  the User Dictionary file any time that it is exited, whether this be through normal exit behaviour (e.g: The user clicking the X button, or terminating their graphical session normally.) However, if Zettlr closes unexpectedly, such as a crash — whether due to Zettlr itself, or the graphical session crashes, it may attempt to write to the User Dictionary file in-which it no longer has proper access to. 

This can corrupt the User Dictionary File, as I have personally experienced just earlier today.

### Reproducing

Consistent reproduction may be difficult within Zettlr itself, however this does not mitigate how severe this specific bug is.
Specific reproduction steps could be achieved however by doing the following:
1. Initiating a graphical session under a user in a Linux system who's home partition is formatted in ecryptfs.
2. Opening Zettlr, and saving some items to the dictionary.
3. Using a privileged sigterm, or task manager program to kill the graphical session. (Simulating a session crash.)

This may _inconsistently_ result in Zettlr attempting to write to the file whilst the session is no longer available, which under ecryptfs will immediately throw errors related to permissions, however it will still overwrite the file.

### Zettlr version

Zettlr 3.6.0 (f3d8800)

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Linux Mint Debian Edition 6

### Additional Information

I use a GTX 1650 SUPER, which due to various reasons I am incapable of replacing at this time. This is how I noticed a graphical session terminate abruptly, to which the TTY output revealed the ecryptfs errors to me.

I immediately noted that the specific errors indicated data loss, and that it was due to permission errors caused by my session being terminated, yet something wrote a file anyway. After further investigation, I found that Zettlr had reset my entire User Dictionary file.

Immediately, this prompted further investigation, so I opened up KATE, and opened up the `user.dic` file in KATE. I added some data to it that followed the standard structure, then closed Zettlr, and it did indeed write to the file as it exited, erasing all of the new entries. 

I then opened up Zettlr to confirm the data was gone, and it was. Then, I closed it down again to write my data once more, this time permanently before opening up Zettlr one last time; my data was there as expected.

Here were the ecryptfs errors immediately after the graphical session terminated abruptly, and Zettlr attempted to write to the `user.dic` file while lacking sufficient access to do so safely:
``` 
ecryptfs_decrypt_page: Error attempting to read lower page; rc = [-4]
ecryptfs_write_begin: Error decrypting page at index [0]; rc = [-4]
ecryptfs_write_metadata_to_contents: Error attempting to write header information to lower file; rc = [-4]
ecryptfs_write_metadata: Error writing metadata out to lower file; rc = [-4]
Error writing headers; rc = [-4]
```

summary: Link incorrectly reports file does not exist

description.text:
### Description

I link an existing note via [[e1906-1910-78085]] which points to an existing file. When I hover over the link it reports "File does not exist". If I then open file, input the file name, and select the file, it will of course open the file.

### Reproducing

This began to happen to me about 3 days ago. Prior to that it hadn't been apparent to me. Now it happens often. I don't know what it different now.

### Zettlr version

Zettlr 3.6.0 UUID: 7f1e155e-93ca-4c36-b780-197d80a0c859

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Mac OS 13.7.8 (22H730)

### Additional Information

There doesn't appear to be anything in the log that directly corresponds in time to my observation of the bug, but, here are a few messages from the log from earlier in the day:

[12:06:10]
[R] [Main Window (656e4a1a-97c3-4f31-a27f-5fb9a22087b9)] Measure loop restarted more than 5 times (index.js:2)
[12:06:54]
[WorkspaceManager] Root /Users/ltalley/Mailboats Project/d1938-1942-78074.md has been removed
[12:29:43]
[R] [Main Window (656e4a1a-97c3-4f31-a27f-5fb9a22087b9)] MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventEmitter memory leak detected. 101 documents-update listeners added. Use emitter.setMaxListeners() to increase limit (sandbox_bundle:2)

### Description

It turns out that there are a few things that make the app less portable than others.

1. Presumably during the installation process the Zettlr Tutorial folder and it's files are created in the Windows User Documents folder
2. There is a "lockfile" that is created in the C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Roaming\Zettlr rather than the portable folder as suggested in the zettlr docs portable page.

### Reproducing

Following the instructions here: https://docs.zettlr.com/en/portable/

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

Perhaps in the installer there should be a dedicated portable option which would create the tutorial within a subfolder of the user chosen installation directory. 
Upon choosing the portable installation the main zettlr.exe should be coded to detect the "zettlr-config" folder and have the "lockfile" moved there on run.

summary: Swap position of the `Files` and `Workspaces` portions of the `File Manager`

description.text:
### Description

As I've begun to use Zettlr more to create edit one-off notes and `.md` files, the list of files in the `Files` section of the file manager has grown unwieldy, whereas my list of workspaces, which are typically dedicated to a handful of important projects, remains compact. Because of this, I have to scroll down quite a ways to reach the `Workspaces` section of the file manager. I'd like the option to swap the position of the two sections of the file manager, so that the `Workspaces` section is up top, and the long list of files is beneath. Then if the files list trails off to infinity, no worries. I can still find my important workspace folders easily up top.

### Proposed Changes

Add a `Show Workspaces section before Files in file manager sidebar` option to the settings menu or simply change the default behavior.

### Caveats

Users may confuse this setting with `Show folders before files` in the settings panel. What I'm referring to is the order of the `Workspaces` and `Files` sections of the file manager, not whether folders are shown up top in the file listings for an individual workspace. Some language in the settings UI to disambiguate the two may be necessary.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

Thank you, as always!

summary: `Tab` should move from the `Find` to the `Replace` field in the `Find in current file` panel.

description.text:
### Description

When I use the `Find in current file` and enter a search term in the `Find` field, I will often, out of habit from other editors and web UIs, hit the `Tab` key to advance my cursor to the `Replace` field. However, unlike in these other apps, doing so does _not_ select the `Replace` field. Instead, it (presumably—no visual feedback is provided) selects the buttons and checkboxes in the panel. Some experimentation shows that hitting `Tab` seven times eventually places my cursor in the `Replace` field.

### Proposed Changes

Reorder the form fields in the HTML source so that the user only needs to hit `Tab` once after typing in the `Find` field to advance their cursor to the `Replace` field.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

Thank you, as always!

summary: Add `Reveal in File Browser` option to the right-click menu for tabs.

description.text:
### Description

In Zettlr's file manager, there's a handy `Reveal in File Browser` option that I use all the time. However, I'll often browse away from a file in the Zettlr file manager and still have it open in a tab. Then, when I want to reveal that file in my system's file browser, I have to navigate back through Zettlr's file manager to find the file listing again.

It would be much easier to simply select `Reveal in File Browser` from the tab's right-click menu if it were also available there.

### Proposed Changes

See above.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

It's relevant that I use the `Thin View` of the file manager, which makes navigating back to a file a process of stepping up and down through project folders. Users of the `Compact` view might not find this as much of a nuisance.

Thanks, as always!

### Description

My informal observation of the Discord channel and my own usage loosely suggest that `Remove line breaks` is probably the most-used of the new text transforms (thank you, again!). As such, I suggest adding a keyboard shortcut for it to make it that much handier.

### Proposed Changes

Some editors used `Ctrl-J` (Join Lines) for this purpose, though that is currently taken in Zettlr by the `Distraction Free Mode`, and in any case, Zettlr doesn't use the "Join Lines" phrasing, so the "J" would not be a good mnemonic.

Perhaps `Ctrl-\`, as the backslash special character is often Unix-speak for "continues on next line."

### Caveats

None.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

Thank you, as always.

summary: Add keyboard shortcut to toggle a task on and off.

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal
__Add a dedicated shortcut to toggle tasks or checkboxes.__

I recently switched to Zettlr because of it's simplicity but a major turn off is that __it doesn't have a dedicated keyboard shortcut to toggle a task/checkbox__.
Having to use the mouse after typing a task is not that fast. And not everyone wants to use the mouse while writing their notes.
Adding a shortcut key for toggling a task will be very helpful.

The combinations can be:

> Ctrl+D
> Ctrl+Shift+T
> F12

Or whatever the devs like.

### Example Workflow

- For those who don't want to use the mouse much while writing, this will be a great feature to manage their to-do list.
- It will be just faster to not have to use the mouse.
- More focused workflow.

### Caveats

I don't think there are any Caveats of this.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description

I think it would be helpful for Zettlr to implement a linting modal window, similar to what Word/LibreOffice show when you do a full document spell check -- The section of text with the highlighted error, a list of suggestions, options to ignore, accept, or add the error to a dictionary, and a button to move to the next or previous error.

The modal could also have options to force lint the document and only show specific errors (only spellchecking, markdown, or languagetool).

Currently, this can be achieved through the diagnostic panel, but its not that ergonomic.

### Proposed Changes

Codemirror offers several helpers that would make the backend fairly trivial, and most work would be to implement the UI.

### Caveats

NA

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

Yes

### Zettlr Version

Right off the develop branch

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

macOS 26, Fedora 42

### Additional Information

_No response_
